definitions: A superstitious plea to avoid causing bad luck by speaking about something positive before it is certain.
An expression used to caution someone against tempting fate by prematurely celebrating or acknowledging a positive outcome.
examples: I know we're close to winning, but don't jinx it!
You just got the job offer, but don't jinx it by telling everyone yet.
We're having a great day, don't jinx it by mentioning anything negative.
